ingredients.address: Technical Park Sakuragi Parking Lot, 2 Chome-281 Sakuragicho, Omiya Ward, Saitama, 330-0854, Japan

ingredients.name: Akihabara Gamers
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-14-7 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Akihabara Gamers flagship store appears in episode 16, where Konata shops for anime merchandise and games. It is a recurring haunt tied to her otaku lifestyle and the show's loving parody of Akiba culture.

ingredients.name: Kenroku-en
ingredients.address: Kenroku-en, 1 Kenrokumachi, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 920-0936, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: One of Japan's top three gardens, Kenroku-en in Kanazawa appears in episode 22 when the cast takes a trip to the city, providing a picturesque backdrop for relaxed conversation between the characters.

ingredients.name: Photo booth shop Kyoto Olympia
ingredients.localName: プリクラショップ 京都オリンピア
ingredients.address: Photo booth shop Kyoto Olympia, 502 Higashigawacho, Nakagyo Ward, Kyoto, 604-8046, Japan

ingredients.name: Animate Kyoto
ingredients.address: Animate Kyoto, パッサージオ（吉本ビル 2F 525-1 Higashigawacho, Nakagyo Ward, Kyoto, 604-8046, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Visited during the Kyoto trip in episode 21, where Konata immediately seeks out the local Animate store rather than traditional sightseeing spots, much to her friends' exasperation.

ingredients.name: Kyoto Animation Co. Ltd.
ingredients.localName: 株式会社京都アニメーション 本社
ingredients.address: Kyoto Animation Co. Ltd., Osedo-32 Kohata, Uji, Kyoto 611-0002
ingredients.entityContext: The studio that produced Lucky Star, visited by the girls in episode 21 during the Kyoto school trip arc. Konata's reverence for the studio is played for comedy as she treats it like a sacred anime pilgrimage site.

ingredients.name: Eburidei Pharmacy
ingredients.entityContext: A pharmacy near the Kyoto Animation studio neighborhood in Uji, visible in episode 21 background shots of the area surrounding the KyoAni headquarters.

ingredients.name: Kowata Station
ingredients.address: Kowata Station, Nishinaka Kohata, Uji, Kyoto 611-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kowata Station on the Kintetsu Kyoto Line near the Kyoto Animation studio headquarters appears in episode 21 during the KyoAni-adjacent Uji sequence, recognizable to fans who follow the pilgrimage route to the studio.

ingredients.name: Kyoto Shiyakusho mae [Daimonjicho]
ingredients.address: Kyoto Shiyakusho mae [Daimonjicho], Oikedaitōchō, Nakagyo Ward, Kyoto, 604-0942, Japan

ingredients.name: Hotel Honnoji
ingredients.address: Hotel Honnōji, 522 Shimohonnojimaecho, Nakagyo Ward, Kyoto, 604-8091, Japan

ingredients.name: Love Fortune Stone
ingredients.address: Love Fortune Stone, 1 Chome-317 Kiyomizu, Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto, 605-0862, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The pair of love-divination stones at Jishu Shrine within Kiyomizu-dera grounds are visited in episode 21. Konata's attempt at the stone walk is played for laughs given her disinterest in romance.

ingredients.name: Kofuku-ji Nan-endo (Southern Round Hall)
ingredients.address: Nanen-do, 48 Noboriojicho, Nara, 630-8213, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The distinctive octagonal Southern Octagonal Hall at Kofuku-ji is seen during the Nara portion of the episode 21 school trip, a recognizable silhouette in the Nara skyline backgrounds.

ingredients.name: Tobu-Dobutsu Koen Station
ingredients.address: Tobu-Dobutsukoen Station, 1-chōme-6 Chūō, Miyashiro, Minamisaitama District, Saitama 345-0821, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tobu-Dobutsukoen Station on the Tobu Skytree Line appears in episode 20 as a transit point in the Saitama local commuter route used by the characters.

ingredients.name: Satte Station
ingredients.address: Satte Station, 1-chōme-1 Naka, Satte, Saitama 340-0115, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Satte Station appears in episode 20 as part of the local Saitama rail corridor, reflecting the show's careful depiction of the real Tobu-line commute the characters take.

ingredients.name: SONIC CITY
ingredients.entityContext: Sonic City, the large civic hall near Omiya Station, appears in episode 15 as a background building in the Omiya city center streetscape that the show meticulously recreates.

ingredients.entityContext: The area near Kasukabe Kyoei High School appears in episode 14 as a background location in the Kasukabe neighborhood scenes, part of the show's detailed recreation of the real local streetscape.

ingredients.name: Married Trees of Akiba-Jinja
ingredients.address: Married Trees of Akiba-Jinja, 1 Chome-42-1 Central, Kasukabe, Saitama 344-0067, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The entwined pine trees at Akiba Shrine in Kasukabe appear in episode 13, a quiet local landmark that adds to the show's affectionate portrayal of the Kasukabe neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Washinomiya Shrine
ingredients.address: Washinomiya Shrine, 1 Chome-6-1 Washinomiya, Kuki, Saitama 340-0217, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Hiiragi family shrine in Lucky Star, where Kagami and Tsukasa's father serves as a priest. It became the single most famous Lucky Star pilgrimage spot, drawing tens of thousands of fans each New Year after the anime aired in 2007.

ingredients.name: Lawson Tokyo Big Sight Entrance
ingredients.localName: ローソン 東京ビッグサイトエントランス店
ingredients.address: Lawson Tokyo Big Sight Entrance, 3 Chome-11-1 Ariake, Koto City, Tokyo 135-0063, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Lawson convenience store at the Big Sight entrance appears in episode 12 during Comiket, where fans line up for refreshments before battling the crowds inside the convention halls.

ingredients.entityContext: Sugito-Takanodai Station features in episode 11 as one of the real Tobu-line stations along the characters' commute route through northern Saitama.

ingredients.address: Miyamae Bridge, 2 Chome Nanukamachi, Takayama, Gifu 506-0851, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Miyamae Bridge in Takayama, Gifu appears in episode 9 as a scenic bridge location. Its appearance in the show drew pilgrimage visitors to the Hida Takayama area.

ingredients.entityContext: The real-world train station that serves as the model for the station Konata and her friends pass through in the Kasukabe area scenes. Kasukabe (fictionalized as Kasukabe in the show) is the hometown of the main cast.

ingredients.address: Mizuho Bank Kasukabe Branch, 1 Chome-1-1 Central, Kasukabe, Saitama 344-0067, Japan

ingredients.address: Otori Chaya, 2-25 Konohanamachi, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 920-0852, Japan

ingredients.name: Senso-ji
ingredients.address: Sensō-ji, 2 Chome-3-1 Asakusa, Taito City, Tokyo 111-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Konata and friends visit Senso-ji in episode 11, with Konata making wishes that predictably revolve around anime and gaming luck. The famous Kaminarimon gate and Nakamise shopping street appear in background shots.

ingredients.name: The Alley - Lumine Omiya
ingredients.localName: THE ALLEY ルミネ大宮店
ingredients.address: The Alley - Lumine Omiya, ルミネ大宮 LUMINE2 1F 630 Nishikicho, Omiya Ward, Saitama, 330-0853, Japan

ingredients.name: Tokyo Big Sight Rooftop Exhibition Area
ingredients.localName: 東京ビッグサイト屋上展示場
ingredients.type: コンベンション センター
categories: コンベンション センター
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒135-0063 Tokyo, Koto City, Ariake, 3 Chome−11−1, 東京国際展示場西展示棟4F
ingredients.entityContext: The outdoor exhibition area of Tokyo Big Sight is shown in episode 12 as part of the Comiket sequence, capturing the massive scale of the event that Konata treats as her most important annual pilgrimage.

ingredients.name: Akihabara Station
ingredients.address: 1 Chome Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Akihabara Station is the gateway to Konata's beloved otaku district, seen in episode 16 as the group arrives for a shopping trip that turns into a tour of anime and game stores.

ingredients.name: Higashiyama Jisho-ji
ingredients.address: 2 Ginkakujicho, Sakyo Ward, Kyoto, 606-8402, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Ginkaku-ji (the Silver Pavilion) is visited by the class in episode 21's Kyoto trip sequence. Konata observes the famous moss garden while spinning comparisons to RPG dungeon aesthetics.

ingredients.name: Yasaka
ingredients.address: 625 Gionmachi Kitagawa, Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto, 605-0073, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Yasaka Shrine is part of the Gion sightseeing route in episode 21, visible as the class strolls through Kyoto's famous Higashiyama district during their school trip.

ingredients.name: Tokyo Big Sight
ingredients.type: コンベンション センター
categories: コンベンション センター
ingredients.address: 3 Chome-11-1 Ariake, Koto City, Tokyo 135-0063, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The convention center depicted in episode 12 when Konata drags her friends to Comiket, the massive doujinshi fair. Konata's obsessive fan-girl energy peaks here as she races to buy limited-edition goods.

ingredients.name: Todai-ji
ingredients.address: 406-1 Zoshicho, Nara, 630-8587
ingredients.entityContext: The Great Buddha Hall of Todai-ji in Nara is visited in episode 21 as the class extends their trip to Nara, with Konata joking that the giant Buddha looks like a boss character from an RPG.

ingredients.name: Sannenzaka
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-211 Kiyomizu, Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto, 605-0862, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The historic stone-paved slope in Higashiyama appears during the Kyoto trip scenes in episode 21, used as a scenic background as the group walks between Kiyomizu-dera and Gion.

ingredients.name: Near the Sanjo Ohashi Bridge
ingredients.address: Ohashicho, Nakagyo Ward, Kyoto, 604-8004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The area near Sanjo Bridge along the Kamogawa River is part of the Kyoto stroll sequence in episode 21, where the girls walk the riverside during their school trip.

ingredients.name: Maruyama Park
ingredients.address: Maruyamacho, Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto, 605-0071, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The park near Yasaka Shrine appears in episode 21 during the Kyoto class trip, providing a pleasant outdoor setting for the group to relax between temple visits.

ingredients.name: Kyoto Station
ingredients.address: Higashishiokoji Kamadonocho, Shimogyo Ward, Kyoto, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kyoto Station serves as the arrival and departure hub for the class trip in episode 21, where the group gathers with luggage before setting out for their Kyoto-Nara sightseeing.

ingredients.name: Kofuku-ji
ingredients.address: 48 Noboriojicho, Nara, 630-8213, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kofuku-ji in Nara appears in the episode 21 school trip arc as the class walks through the deer park area near its famous five-story pagoda.

ingredients.name: Kiyomizu-dera
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-294 Kiyomizu, Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto, 605-0862, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Featured in episode 21 during the Kyoto class trip, where Konata compares the iconic wooden stage to scenes from video games. The landmark view is a backdrop for the group's light-hearted sightseeing banter.

ingredients.name: Gongendo Tsutsumi
ingredients.address: 887-3 Uchigouma, Satte, Saitama 340-0103, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The cherry-blossom embankment in Satte appears in episode 15 as a springtime outing backdrop, one of the Saitama nature spots the show uses to ground its slice-of-life mood in recognizable local scenery.

ingredients.name: Animate Akihabara
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-3-1 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Akihabara branch of Animate is one of the anime retail stops Konata frequents in episode 16, reinforcing the show's portrayal of Akihabara as Konata's natural habitat for buying manga, doujinshi, and figures.
